def: s.m. Flour or powder made from the fruit of the ber or Rhamnus jujuba.
adj. Put to flight, routed, overcome by an enemy or by misfortune, crying for quarter or mercy; — intj. Mercy! help! save us!
s.f. Brigade.
s.m. Separation, &c. = birah, q.v.; a kind of song peculiar to washermen.
s.m. Land in which culinary herbs are grown.
adj. & s.m. Suffering the pangs of absence from a beloved one, love-sick; amorous, sensual; — one who is love-sick, &c.
s.f. A flake of pān or betel for eating, a leaf of pān or betel folded up into a triangular form with pieces of areca nut, spices, chunam, and cardamoms inside of it ( = bīṛā; syn. khillī).
s.f. = bīṛī, q.v.; — the colour which adheres to the lips from chewing betel; a composition which, being rubbed on the teeth, stains their interstices of a reddish colour.
s.m. Galbanum (P. bireza).
s.m. Rice (freed from the husk): birinj-ārī, s.m. One who follows a camp with grain; grain-merchant, grain-carrier, suttler ( = banjārā)
subs: birinj-phūl, s.m. A species of rice.

def: s.m. Brass (syn. pītal).
s.m. See s.v. birinj, 'rice.'
adj. & s.m. Made of brass, brazen; — a small nail, tack (syn. kokā).
part. Parched, roasted, fried, broiled.
s.f. Ear-ornaments (of a woman).
s.f. Name of a class of harlots.
s.f. = biriyāṅ, q.v.
s.f. Time, space of time
subs: Once, one time; at one time; once upon a time.

ety: the Pers. barjīs arabicized: — cf. S. gen. br̤ihas- in br̤ihas-pati
def: s.m. The planet Jupiter (syn. qāẓī-ě-falak).
s.m. One of the subdivisions of ahīrs, q.v.
s.m. Pond; small well; basin of a fountain.
s.f. = baṛāṛ or biṛār, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Few, little; scarce, uncommon, &c. ( = biral, q.v.); — an odd, eccentric, or perverse person.
v.t. To cause to stop or desist, to stop; to reduce to obedience or subjection, to subdue, tame; — to play with, allure, tantalize, tease.
adj. Taming; tamed; allured, tantalized.
s.m. A subdivision of the bhāṭ or bard tribe.

ety: S. वरेण, or भृङ्ग; cf. bhiṛ
def: s.f. Wasp.
s.f. A small seed or grain.
s.m. Small tree, sapling.
s.m. Contrariety, difference, variance, opposition, obstruction, hostility, enmity; quarrel, contention, dispute
adj. & s.m. Contentious, quarrelsome, antagonistic, obstructive; — opponent, antagonist, enemy; a quarrelsome person.
s.m. Separation, disunion, loss, absence (especially of lovers; — syn. birak; judāʼī; hijr).
adj. & s.f. Separated, grieved or distressed through separation (from her husband or lover); a wife who mourns the absence of her husband.
adj. & s.m. Separated, disjoined, apart, absent; — a man who is parted or separated from his beloved; a lover suffering the pain of absence.
s.m. A fox-cub.
s.m. Grain and barley sown in the same field
s.m. = برا birrā, q.v.
s.m. Substance, means, potency, power, ability, force, virtue, excellence; action, deed, achievement; help, support
subs: (-ke), To rejoice, or to plume or pride oneself, on the substance, power, or support (of).

def: s.f. Heroism, prowess, valour.
s.m. = H بڙتي बिड़ती biṛtī, s.f. = birtā, q.v.
s.f. Name of a plant, a species of Panicum.
s.f. = H بڙتا बिड़ता biṛtā, s.m. = birtā, q.v.
s.m. A. tenant who holds his land on a fixed unalterable annual assessment (cf. birt).
s.m. colloq. form of bīr, q.v.
s.m. Panegyrist, bard; — archer, bowman.

part. adj. & postp. (as a part. it governs the abl.; as a postp. the gen. ke); opposite, opposed (to), the opposite (of), contrary (to), against, discordant, at variance; contrariwise (syn. bar-ḵẖilāf).
s.f. Opposition, contrariety, incongruity; aversion, enmity, hostility.
adv. Without, outside; externally; — s.m. The outside.
adj. & s.m. Misshapen, deformed, disfigured, ugly, monstrous, hideous, unnatural; — difference of nature; deformity, ugliness, monstrosity; opposition, dislike
subs: v.n. To be disfigured, &c.; to be disgraced.

def: s.m. The labourer employed at the basket used in raising water for irrigation (cf. bāhrā; bārā).
s.m. Child, boy.

ety: for pirwā, dim. of peṛ: wā = S. uka
def: s.m. A small or young tree; tree; plant.
s.f. Enclosure, hedge (of thorns, &c.) surrounding a field or garden, fence; garden, orchard, plantation
subs: v.n. To enclose with a hedge or fence; to make an orchard or plantation.

def: s.m. Valour, heroism (bīrj).
adj. Fried, roasted, parched, grilled, broiled; burning, consuming, &c.
s.f. Name of a dish made of meat and rice.
s.m. Poison, venom, bane ( = zahr); the vegetable poison Aconitum ferox (syn. baćnāg); (met.), anything bitter, disagreeable, spiteful, &c.
subs: v.n. lit. 'To vomit venom'; to say venomous or spiteful things (of), speak ill (of), malign, revile, sneer at; to disburden the mind or the conscience; to have (one's) say; to take revenge
adj. & s.m. Poisonous; a venomous serpent; snake
v.n. To swallow or take poison

bis-khoprā (S. visha+kharpara+kah), s.m. lit. 'Poison-headed'; the medicinal plant Trianthema pentandra, or T. obcordatum; the plant Mezoneurum cucullatum (Desfontaines), or Cæsalpinia cucullata (Roxb.); — a species of lizard, about half a yard long, which is generally regarded as venomous, Lacerta iguana
bis-khān, bis-gāṅṭh, s.m.f. Viper, mischievous person, devil, demon
(-meṅ), To mingle poison (with), to poison, embitter, corrupt, vitiate, spoil

def: adj. Twenty; — superior (to), more excellent
subs: s.m. lit. 'Having twenty arms'; the monster Rāvaṇ
s.m., adj. & adv. The whole village; full amount; full crop; — whole, complete, total; — on the whole; in all probability, very likely; surely, certainly
s.m. Scores; endless number; — adj. Numberless, countless; — in twenties; in innumerable quantities, in countless numberr.

def: s.m. A score; — an animal (commonly a dog) which has twenty nails.
adj. White, clean, pure, chaste
s.f. Buying, purchasing; incurring; thing bought, purchase.
adj. & s.m. bisahā, q.v.
adj. Poisonous, venomous (reptile, &c.); — s.m. Venomous reptile; — a dog with poisonous teeth and twenty nails.
s.f. = bisāh; and bisāhand; q.q.v.
s.f. Smell of raw meat; fishy smell; rank or offensive smell, stench, stink, fetidness, rankness; coarseness, baseness, vulgarity.
adj. Having a fishy smell;: melling offensive or rank, stinking, rank, fetid; stale, dry, vapid, tasteless.
v.t. To get possession of, procure, acquire; to buy, purchase; to incur, bring upon oneself.
s.f. Venomous reptile; snake; a phlegmon or boil on the hand (syn. bisailā).

r: bisahrī, bisahrū, bisahrūʼā, bisāh, kara, uka
def: s.m. Buyer, purchaser.

r: bisahrū, bisahrūʼā, bisahrī, bisāh, kara, uka
def: s.m. Buyer, purchaser.

r: bisahrūʼā, bisahrū, bisahrī, bisāh, kara, uka
def: s.m. Buyer, purchaser.
s.f. = bisahrī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Poisonous, venomous (syn. zahrīlā); — venomous reptile; snake; phlegmon or boil on the hand (bisahrī).
s.f. = bisāhand, q.v.
s.f. = bisāhand, q.v.
s.f. The name of the sixteenth asterism or lunar mansion (figured by a decorated gateway, and containing four, or originally two, stars).
adj. Large, great, &c. = baṛā, q.v.
s.f. Largeness, &c. = baṛāpan, q.v.
v.n. To be broken, be shattered (see bismānā).
s.f. = bisāhand, q.v.
s.m. = bishn, bishan, q.v.
s.m. Fault, vice, crime, bad practice, evil habit; inordinate addiction (to), desire, lust
v.t. = bisārnā, and bisāhnā, q.q.v.
s.f. = bisāhand, q.v.
adj. = bisāhandā, q.v.
s.f. = bisāhand, q.v.
s.m. Viciousness, dissoluteness, lewdness; habit of indulging desire.
adj. = bisāl, q.v.; — s.f. Loan of seed upon the stipulation of an ample refund after the harvest.
Gleanings left in the field for the lower orders to gather.
s.m. Forgetfulness, oblivion; oversight, omission; — postp. By the omission (of), by neglect (of), failing (in), lacking.
adj. Learned, wise; skilful, skilled (in), versed (in), conversant (with); famous, celebrated; confident, bold, presuming.
s.f. = bisāt, q.v.

s.m. Sending forth, letting go, emission; voiding, evacuation; getting rid of, sending away, dismissal, abandonment, relinquishment; departure, separation; gift, donation; final emancipation, beatitude; light, lustre, splendour; (in Gram.) a symbol usually marked by two dots one over the other (:) representing a hard and distinctly audible aspiration
subs: s.f. (in Gram.), the union of bisarg with a vowel and a consonant (according to special rules) for the formation of a compound letter.

def: s.m. Relinquishing, sending away, dismissing, dismissal; throwing the image of a deity into holy water (as the concluding rite of a festival); finish, end, or conclusion of a religious ceremony; giving away, gift, donation.
v.n. To be forgotten; to forget, lose the remembrance (of); to be unmindful (of).
v.t. To cause to be forgotten; to make (one) forget; to efface, or dismiss, from the memory, to forget ( = bisrānā).
adj. = biswāsī, q.v.
adj. = biswāsī, q.v.

ety: S. विस्टत; or विस्तृत; cf. A. bisāt̤
def: s.f. lit. 'That which is spread out'; goods, wares, stock-in-trade; capital, substance, means, power; ability; significance, importance, moment, weight.
s.f. Anything that is spread out; surface, expanse, expansion; carpet; bedding; chess-cloth or chess-board, dice-board; — goods, wares, &c. ( = bisāt, q.v.).
s.m. Vender of small wares (so called because he spreads out his wares); pedlar, huckster (syn. pherī-wālā, box-wālā, paikār, ḵẖụrda-farosh).
s.m. = bisātī, q.v.
s.f. = bisāhan, or bisāh, q.v.
s.f. = bisāhand, q.v.
s.m. = biswās, q.v.
s.m. Call made to a cat; puss-puss.
v.n. To give forth a sound as in fermentation or putrefaction; to be in a state of fermentation, or putrefaction; to ferment ( = baj-bajānā); to smart, tingle.
s.m. = biśesh, q.v.
s.m. = biśesh, q.v.
s.m. Name of a powerful tribe of Rājpūts.
s.f. Abundance = biśeshatā, q.v.
s.m. = biśesh, q.v.

s.m. Difference, distinction, individuality, characteristic difference, peculiar mark, particular circumstance, special property, speciality, peculiarity, attribute; distinction, peculiar merit, excellence, superiority; sort, kind, manner; (in Gram.) a defined or qualified substantive (syn. mauṣūf); (in Law), special rule or distinction; (in Med.) change for the better; — adj. Distinguished, definite, particular, special, peculiar, distinctive; certain, choice, excellent, superior; abundant, excessive; — adv. Especially, particularly, specially
subs: biśesh-kar-ke, adv. More especially, particularly, par excellence.

def: adj. & s.m. Discriminative, distinctive, attributive; — distinguishing mark, distinction, attribute, characteristic, epithet; (in Gram.), the qualifying word, adjective, adverb ( = kriyā-viśeshan).
s.f. Peculiarity, speciality; abundance, plenty.
s.m. Poison, &c. = bis, q.v.
subs: s.f. lit. 'Poison-damsel'; a female who has been fed on poisons from her infancy. (Such damsels, it is said, were formerly sent by Indian princes as presents to those whom they wished to destroy, as the poisonous constitution of the girl could immediately infect and kill an unwary lover.)

def: adj. = bisahā, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Busied with worldly matters; addicted to worldly pleasures; worldly, sensual, carnal; — an epithet of Kām-deʼo, the Indian Cupid; anyone devoted to sensual or worldly objects; sensualist, voluptuary, debauchee, rake ( = bisnī, q.v.).
adj. = bisailā, q.v.

adj. Uneven, rough, difficult of access; unequal, irregular; not even, odd (number); difficult, hard; disagreeable, painful, troublesome, vexatious; odd, unusual, unequalled (in a bad sense); intermittent, inconstant; bad, adverse, unpropitious
subs: bisham-jwar, s.m. Irregular fever, irregularly remittent fever
s.m. Unequal four-sided figure, trapezium, quadrilateral
adj. Unequal angled; — bisham-koṇ āyat, s.m. Parallelogram; rhomboid; — bisham-koṇ sam-ćatur-bhūj, s.m. Rhombus.

def: s.m. Object of sense; object of concern or attention, worldly object or aim; object, pursuit, affair, concern, business, transaction; matter, topic, subject, region, sense; worldly or sensual enjoyment, sensuality, lust; — postp. In the matter (of, -ke), in respect (of), respecting, concerning, about ( = bābat)
subs: In this matter; in this respect.

def: adj. & s.m. Busied with worldly matters; addicted to worldly pleasures; worldly, sensual, carnal; — an epithet of Kām-deʼo, the Indian Cupid; anyone devoted to sensual or worldly objects; sensualist, voluptuary, debauchee, rake ( = bisnī, q.v.).
adj. = bisyar; bisailā, q.q.v.
s.m. Name of one of the three great divisions of modern Hindū sects; a member of that sect; a worshipper of Vishnu.
s.m. = bishnawī, q.v.
s.m. = bishayī and bisnī, q.q.v.
s.m. A Hindū god, the Deity in the character of Preserver
subs: (S. vishṇu+padya), s.m. A particular song in the name of Vishnu
s.f. Land granted rent-free to Brāhmans in honour of Vishnu, or to maintain his worship
s.m. The holder of a bishn-prīt, or grant of land for religious purposes
s.m. A sect of mendicants who beg in the name of Vishnu.

def: s.m. A provincial term (in Kamāʼūn) for a kind of taʻalluqdār whose office is in the gift of government.
s.f. Ordure, excrement, filth, dirt.
s.f. Rain.
s.f. Strip of cloth (to cover the privities with); shred, scrap.
s.f. = bīsī, q.v.
s.f. A score; a certain measure of grain; a land-measure equivalent to twenty nalīs.
s.m. Arrow, iron crow.
s.m. = biswa-karmā, q.v.s.v. biswa.
s.m. See s.v. bis.
s.m. See s.v. bis.
v.t. To break to pieces, shatter, destroy (a word peculiar to Hindū women).
s.m. Name of a plant.
s.m. Astonishment, amazement, surprise, perplexity, bewilderment, dismay
adj. Astonished, taken by surprise, amazed, disconcerted, perplexed, bewildered, dismayed, afraid.
adj. Sacrificed, slaughtered
subs: v.t. To sacrifice; to slaughter (an animal)

def: adj. For sacrifice; for slaughter.
intj. God be with you! God speed you! very good! very well! all right!
subs: In the name of God the Merciful, the Compassionate
v.n. To pronounce the formula 'in the name of God,' at the beginning of a work, etc.; (hence) to begin, commence; to fall to (eating), set to work; to slaughter (an animal)
v.n. To abide under the dome of security, live in peace and safety; to retire from the world, lead a life of seclusion
'A beginning has been made at his lessons' (said of a child who has just commenced learning to read).

def: part. Amazed, &c. = bismayī, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Vicious, dissolute, addicted to any kind of vice or evil practice; delicate, nice; showy in dress (syn. sughaṛ); — dissolute man, voluptuary, libertine, debauchee, rake, roué, lecher (cf. bishnī).
adj. Free from grief or sorrow, happy.
s.m. Twenty days of an intercalary month (for which it is the custom among bohrās or money-lenders to cut off the interest); the interest so cut off.
adj. Forgetful, oblivious; negligent.
s.f. Forgetfulness, &c. = bis̤ar, q.v.
s.m. Rest, repose, quiet, ease, cessation from labour or fatigue; pause, stop, point in writing
subs: bisrām lenā, v.n. To rest, repose, &c.; to pass the night (particularly used by wandering faqīrs).

def: s.m. = bisrām, q.v.
v.t. To cause to forget; to forget ( = bisārnā), to efface from the memory; to distract, beguile; to mislead (syn. bhulānā; bahkānā).
part. Rested, reposed.
part. adj. = bisrānt, q.v.
s.f. Rest, repose, &c. = bisrām, q.v.
s.f. = bisrāhaṭ, q.v.
adj. Twenty ( = bīs, q.v.).
s.m. A provincial term (in Kamāʼūn) for a kind of taʻalluqdār whose office is in the gift of government.
adj. Twenty.
s.m. Spreading out; spread, extension, expansion, diffusion, development, amplification, detail, prolixity, copiousness, abundance; (in Geom.) diameter of a circle
subs: (-kā), To spread, extend, enlarge, expand, diffuse, develop, &c.

def: s.m. Bedding, mattress (cf. bistarā); carpet; bed, bolster, pillow.
s.m. Bed, bedding (particularly of a soldier, faqīr, traveller, &c., which is rolled up in the morning); the abode or resting place of a faqīr (cf. takiya)
subs: bistarā jamānā, bistarā lagānā, v.n. To spread or lay out the bedding, make ready the bed.

def: s.m. Bed, bedding (particularly of a soldier, faqīr, traveller, &c., which is rolled up in the morning); the abode or resting place of a faqīr (cf. takiya)
subs: bistarā jamānā, bistarā lagānā, v.n. To spread or lay out the bedding, make ready the bed.

def: adj. Extending, large, ample, diffuse.
v.t. To spread, extend, &c. = bistār karnā, q.v.s.v. bistār.
s.f. The house-lizard (sy. n ćhipkalī; pallī).
s.f. The house-lizard (syn. ćhipkalī; pallī).
v.n. To sob, &c. = bisūrnā, q.v.
v.n. To put on a crying face, to prepare to weep; to cry slowly; to sob; to pull a long face.
see بسوه biswa.

ety: Ap. Prk. वीसवँओ = Prk. वीसमओ = S. विंशम+कः
def: s.m. Twentieth part; a measure of land, the twentieth part of a bīghā
subs: s.m., biswāṅsī, s.f. (i.e. biswā+ansa = S. anśa+kah), Twentieth part of a biswā
s.m. Collecting, or collection, by the biswā
s.m. Holder of a share or shares in a coparcenary village; — biswā-dārī, s.f. Coparcenary holding or tenure; tenure of independent village communities under a superior taʻallug-dār.

def: s.m. = biswā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. All, every, every one, entire, whole, universal; — whole world, all creation, universe
subs: s.m. lit. 'All-maker'; name of the artificer or architect of the gods, the Vulcan of Hindu mythology; the god of artisans (especially of carpenters); — an astute or crafty person.

def: s.m. See s.v. biswa.
adj. Twentieth
subs: adv. In the twentieth place, twentiethly.

def: s.f. See s.v. biswā.
s.m. (?), Mixture of spices and aromatic seeds powdered and ready for use in curry or pickle; curry-powder.
s.m. Trust, confidence, faith, belief, reliance, dependence
subs: biswās karnā (-kā; or -par), To put trust (in), to confide (in), rely (on), to trust, believe, credit
s.f. Destruction of confidence, breach of faith, violation of trust, treachery, treacherous friendship; — biswās-ghātī, s.m. One who betrays confidence, traitor, treacherous friend.

def: s.m. Corr. of A. waswās, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. biswāsin, -inī), Confiding, faithful, believing; honest, trusty; — truster, believer; confidant.
adj. Of or relating to the world, worldly, belonging to the universe.
s.f. Alienation of land on low jamaʻs on the payment of fines in advance.
adj. Much, many, very, abundant.
adj. & s.m. = bisailā, q.v.
s.f. Abundance, superfluity.
s.f. Grazing fee charged by herdsmen, demand on each head of cattle paid to the cowherd, a kind of agistment
subs: s.m. Field or land grazed on by agistment; — land cultivated by forced labour.

def: s.f. = bīṭh, q.v.

ety: S. विट, rt. विट्; or विड
def: s.f. A factitious salt containing sulphur ( = biṭ-lavaṇ).
s.f. = bīt, q.v.
s.m. Span = bittā, q.v.
v.t. To spread, &c. = bithārnā, biṭānā, biṭlānā, q.q.v.; to put (food) into (the mouth), to taste (food); e.g. muṅh biṭālnā.
s.m. Awning, canopy, &c. = vi-tān, q.v.
v.t. To spread, &c. = bithrānā, biṭālnā, q.q.v.
v.t. To cause to pass, to pass, spend (time).
wety: From Sanskrit वत् (vat) + ـآ (-ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: s.m. Young branch of a tree, new shoot or sprout; branch; tree, bush; clump, cluster, tuft; — spreading, expansion.
s.m. Spreading, extension, &c. = vi-stār, q.v.
s.m. Giving up, abandoning; donation, gift, charity, alms.
v.t. To give away, bestow, grant.
s.m. Little son, &c. = biṭwā, q.v.
s.m. = biṭaurā, q.v.
s.m. Heap of cakes of dried cowdung (used for fuel).
s.f. A tax on shopkeepers, &c. in a village.
s.f. Excrement, dung (especially of birds).
s.f. Disquietude, agitation; pain, agony; anguish, ache; distress, misery, trouble.
s.m. Annular cushion put on the head to carry a pot of water, &c. (syn. bīṅḍā; inḍwā).
s.m. Ant-hill.
v.n. To be alarmed, scared, &c. = bijhaknā, q.v.
v.t. = biṭhānā, q.v. (cf. also the forms biṭhlānā, baiṭhānā, baiṭhālnā).

v.t. To cause to enter; to enter, place (in, -meṅ, as a boy in a school); to pierce, penetrate, thrust in, drive home, press down, ram down, pack closely, stuff, fix firmly; — to cause to be seated, cause to sit, to seat; to settle; to set (as a stone in a ring, &c.), to plant, sow; to cause to congeal, coagulate, solidify; to cause to cake, form into lumps; to locate, station, lodge; to instal; to establish, fix, set up; to cause to settle down; to lay (as dust, &c.), to allay, calm, assuage; to set (over, -par, as a watch, guard, &c.); to fix or impress (on, -meṅ, the mind), to implant (in the mind), impress (the mind with an idea), strike (terror, &c. into the mind); to set in order, settle, arrange, adjust; to make out (a calculation, &c.), strike (a balance); to establish, get up, cause to be convened (a panćāyat, &c.); to come into possession of, gain, realize, gather, collect; to make (the heart) sink or faint (dil biṭhānā)
subs: v.t. (intensive of biṭhānā, but often used in the sense of biṭhānā), To cause to fall in, cause to sink or founder; to demolish, destroy, raze, level, ruin; to melt, dissolve; to settle, knock down, stun, &c. (with, -meṅ, a blow); to stake, bet (upon, -par); to gamble away, lose
v.t. To keep seated; to detain; to keep (a marriageable or married daughter) at home; to refuse to give in marriage; — biṭhā-lenā, v.t. To make (one) sit = biṭhānā (generally in an intensive sense).

def: s.f. Brushing or cleansing the warp ( = bithrī).

ety: bithār˚ = Prk. वित्थारे(इ) = S. विस्तारय(ति), caus. of rt. वि+स्तृ
def: v.t. To spread, scatter, &c. ( = bithrānā, q.v.; and see also bitharnā, of which it is the caus.).
v.n. To be spread, sown, scattered; to be sprinkled, splashed, &c.
v.t. To cause to be spread, &c.
s.f. Row, line; road; terrace in front of a house; stall, shop; — a sort of drama; a division of the planetary spheres (comprising three asterisms).
v.t. = biṭhānā, q.v.
v.n. (local) = baiṭhnā, q.v.
v.t. To spread, scatter, strew; to sprinkle; to bespatter; to splash, spill; to waste.
s.f. = bithārī, q.v.
part. adj. Passed, elapsed, gone, spent, bygone
v.n. To pass, elapse, &c. ( = bitīt honā, bītnā); to happen (to), to betide.
s.f. Little daughter; daughter.
s.m. Black salt, a particular kind of fetid salt (obtained by fusing fossile salt with emblic myrobalans, and used medicinally).
s.m. A coarse species of pulse (of the māsh, or of the kalāya kind).
v.n. To pass; to come to pass, &c. = bītnā, q.v.

ety: bīt˚ = Prk. वित्ते(इ), or वित्त(इ), fr. वित्त = S. वीत, p.p.p. rt. वी
def: v.n. To pass, elapse, expire (as time); to come to pass, take place, occur, happen, befall, arrive; to undergo, suffer, endure
subs: v.n. To pass away, pass over, &c.

ety: bīṭ˚ = Prk. विट्टे(इ), or विट्ट(इ), fr. विट्ट (for विट्ठ) = S. व्यस्त, p.p.p. of rt. वि+अस
def: v.t. To pour out, spill; to scatter.
s.f. = bhiṭnī, q.v.
v.t. To scatter, &c. = biṭānā, q.v. (cf. the forms ḍubānā and ḍubonā).
s.f. Detailed settlement = bila-bandī, q.v.
v.t. To bespatter, &c. ( = bithrānā, q.v.); to asperse, calumniate, vilify, denounce; to belie.
s.m. Wealth, riches, property, possessions, money, substance, thing; power, ability, means, resources
subs: adj. Out of (one's) power, beyond (one's) means.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بت (bat, “mark, score”).

def: s.m. Span, hand's breadth.
adv. Explicitly, in detail, &c. ( = biʼt-taṣrīḥ); minutely, item by item, particularly.
adv. Especially, particularly, specifically.
adv. Little by little, bit by bit, piecemeal, in detail; by degrees; gradually.
adv. In detail; expressly, distinctly, explicitly.
s.f. A boys' game (a boy throws forward a small round potsherd with his foot, which another boy has to bring back with his breath held, and crying bittī, bittī); a run.
s.f. Pair, couple, match
subs: v.n. To couple, match, form pairs.

def: s.m. The melon ( = ḵẖarbuza); the water-melon, Cucurbita citrullus.
s.m. lit. 'A span high'; a dwarf (syn. bālishtiyā).

ety: S. वटु+कः; or dim. of beṭā
def: s.m. Little son; dear son; little boy, child.
v.t. To cause to be measured; to have (one) measured or fitted.
s.m. Marriage ( = byāh, q.v.
s.f. Kibe or chap on the heel; chilblain
subs: v.n. To suffer from kibes, or from chilblains.

def: s.m. = bimān, q.v.
s.f. Judicial decree according to Hindū law ( = bewasthā; fatwā).
s.f. Lady ( = bībī, q.v.); an epithet of Fātima, the daughter of Mohammad (to whom Mohammadan women make offerings which none but a chaste woman dare touch).
(imperat. of āmadan), Come
subs: Come! come! (a mode of calling pigeons, falcons, &c.).

def: s.f. Wind ( = bayār).
imperat. (2nd. pers. sing. of āvardan), Bring thou.
s.m. (?), Land cultivated to be sown in the following year.
s.m. Separation (esp. of lovers); unrequited love ( = bijog).
s.m. A lover suffering the pain of absence.
s.f. Merchandise; an article of merchandise; stock-in-trade, capital; agency, commission.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بِضَاعَت (bizā'at), from Arabic بِضَاعَة (biḍāʕa).

def: s.m. General slaughter
subs: v.t. To slay, slaughter, put to the sword.

def: adv. By its own essence; of itself; of himself; in person, personally; essentially; independently; intrinsically.
s.m. A garment that is worn in daily service or work, and that is not taken care of.
adv. Of necessity, necessarily, inevitably, perforce; of course, certainly, positively.
adv. Of necessity, necessarily, inevitably, perforce; of course, certainly, positively.
adv. Essentially, naturally, originally; — biʼz-ẕātihi, In its or his nature or essence.
s.f. Sowing, cultivation
v.t. To cause to smell, to perfume (syn. basānā; gandhānā); — v.n. To emit a smell, to smell, stink.
s.m. The sowing of grain by the drill.
s.m. Goods and chattels, property; bundle.
s.m. Alligator, crocodile (syn. magar; kumbhīr).

ety: from S. rt. कुच् with अव
def: s.m. A chair-pālkī, a kind of sedan (syn. jhappān).
adj. Chipped, jagged, notched (on the edge), serrated, denticulated; gapped (as the teeth).
s.m. (f.?), Distance, remoteness (syn. dūrī).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بعد (ba'd), from Arabic بَعْد (baʕd).

def: adj. Weak, feeble; soft, faint-hearted, low-spirited, timid; low, mean, trifling, trivial, worthless; — s.m. A buffalo.
s.m. A square enclosure of mud in which water is taken for irrigation; a a place to stand on for throwing the daurī or basket by which water is raised from a well.
adj. Intelligent, sensible, ingenious.
s.m. Understanding, knowledge, wisdom, intelligence; perception, apprehension, cognition, conception, thought.
s.m. A security.
adj. & s.m. Causing to know, informing, teaching; — teacher; informer, spy.
s.m. Perception by the understanding, comprehension; knowledge; teaching, informing; arousing, awakening; — incense burning.
s.f. = bodhan, q.v.
part. Made known, taught; expressed; apprized.

ety: bodh˚ = Prk. बोधे(इ), or बोध(इ) = S. बोधय(ति), caus. of rt. बुध्
def: v.t. To learn, to commit to memory; — to wheedle, cajole, coax; to flatter (syn. phuslānā; bhulānā).

ety: S. मृदुलः, or मुग्ध+लः; cf. bodā
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Simple, artless, innocent, soft, silly, foolish; — simpleton, a soft; — bodlī, s.f. Simple woman, trollop; a woman who lives with a be-nawā faqīr, and dresses like a man.
s.m. Outer covering, wrapper; pouch
subs: s.m. Cloth in which anything is wrapped up.

def: s.m. Trumpery, trifles, scraps, rags, &c.; the stores of a wallet (cf. balā-bog̠mā); — adj. f. Ugly and fat (woman).
s.m. A dish made of boiled meat, flour, &c.
v.t. To set afloat ( = bahānā, q.v.); to send away; to separate.
s.m. Vessel, ship, boat.

s.f. Handsel; first sale for ready money early in the day (considered as a good omen and as determining the luck of the day. — The etymology is doubtful: some trace the word to bhawānī, because ascribed to the auspices of that goddess; others derive it from the root vr̤idh).
s.f. = bohnī, q.v.
s.m. A man who (having no plough of his own) works with a borrowed plough, spade, &c.

ety: S. व्यवहारकः, rt. हृ, with वि and अअव
def: s.m. A class of village bankers or money-lenders; an individual of that class. (The Bohrās are Mohammadans, who came originally from Guzrāt)
s.m. Crisis of a disease.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُحْرَان (buhrān), from Arabic بُحْرَان (buḥrān).

def: s.m. False accusation, calumny, slander, aspersion, defamation
subs: (-par), bǒhtān joṛnā, bǒhtān dharnā, bǒhtān lagānā (-par), To bring a false accusation (against), charge falsely; to slander, calumniate.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُهْتَان (buhtān), borrowed from Arabic بُهْتَان (buhtān), from بَهَتَ (bahata).

def: s.f. Sowing, cultivation
subs: s.m. Assessment to be realized on cultivation.

def: s.m. A small basket (such as seed is carried in when sowing); a work-basket.

ety: S. ऊढः, or वोढ, or अपोढ; rt. वह्
def: s.m. Load, burden, bundle (of grain, or grass, &c.); weight; load of grain in the straw (as much as can be carried on the head); ballast (of a boat or ship); cargo, lading, freight; weight, importance, gravity; incumbrance, drag, onus, difficulty burden, responsibility, obligation
subs: (-kā), To put down a load or burden; to help (one) to set down; load, &c.; to throw off, or to relieve (oneself) of a burden, incumbrance, or responsibility to perform or discharge a duty (generally in a perfunctory way)
v.n. To take up, or to bear, a load, &c.; to take a burden, or responsibility upon oneself
s.f. A mode of distribution by stacks or bundles of cut corn
(-kā), To assume or affect a degree of importance or consequence; to give oneself airs
(-kā), To be labouring under a burden, or difficulty; to be under an obligation: bojh-kan, s.m. Material particle
v.n. To load (on, -par), lade; to emburden, encumber
(-ke), To groan and sweat under a burden.

ety: S. ऊढः, or वोढ, or अपोढ; rt. वह्
def: s.m. Load, burden, bundle (of grain, or grass, &c.); weight; load of grain in the straw (as much as can be carried on the head); ballast (of a boat or ship); cargo, lading, freight; weight, importance, gravity; incumbrance, drag, onus, difficulty burden, responsibility, obligation
subs: (-kā), To put down a load or burden; to help (one) to set down; load, &c.; to throw off, or to relieve (oneself) of a burden, incumbrance, or responsibility to perform or discharge a duty (generally in a perfunctory way)
v.n. To take up, or to bear, a load, &c.; to take a burden, or responsibility upon oneself
s.f. A mode of distribution by stacks or bundles of cut corn
(-kā), To assume or affect a degree of importance or consequence; to give oneself airs
(-kā), To be labouring under a burden, or difficulty; to be under an obligation: bojh-kan, s.m. Material particle
v.n. To load (on, -par), lade; to emburden, encumber
(-ke), To groan and sweat under a burden.

def: s.f. Lading, cargo ( = bojhā, q.v.).
adj. = bojhal, and bujhail, qq.v.
adj. Loaded, laden, heavily laden; heavy, ponderous, massive.
v.t. To scald, or parch, rice.
v.t. To load.
